Argentina are the model's clear favorite at 78 percent, while Jordan's win probability stands at just 5 percent. The underdog retains a modest but genuine chance of a draw at 18 percent, suggesting Argentina will not be overwhelmingly dominant despite their advantage in the forecast.

The gap between Argentina's win probability and Jordan's reflects a substantially uneven matchup. A draw remains plausible—nearly one in five outcomes—indicating that while Argentina are preferred, the match retains competitive texture rather than amounting to a coronation. The median outcome tilts sharply toward an Argentina victory, but Jordan's draw probability is meaningful enough to prevent the model from treating this as a rout.

The model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus rates Argentina slightly higher at 80 percent versus the model's 78 percent, while the market is more skeptical of a draw at 13 percent compared to the model's 18 percent. These gaps are modest and fall within typical variance between sources. No Polymarket consensus has yet formed.
